# Tool Reference

<!-- Generated by `just docs-gen` (cmd/toolsdoc). Do not edit by hand. -->

76 tools with a single Pi-hole configured; 78 with multiple instances.

With more than one Pi-hole configured, every tool also accepts an optional `instance` parameter (an instance name, or `all` to aggregate across instances), and the tools under [Multi-Instance](#multi-instance) become available.

## Dashboard

### `pihole_padd`

*read-only · structured output*

One-call dashboard: queries, blocking, top domain/client, cache, versions, and host health. Best starting point for a status overview.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `detail` | string | No | Response detail: minimal, normal (default), or full. |

## DNS Blocking

### `pihole_dns_get_blocking`

*read-only · structured output*

Get the current DNS blocking status and any active timer for temporary blocking changes.

_No parameters._

### `pihole_dns_set_blocking`

*destructive*

Enable or disable DNS blocking. Set a timer (seconds) to automatically revert after a duration.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `blocking` | boolean | Yes | True to enable blocking, false to disable. |
| `timer` | number | No | Seconds until automatic revert. Omit for permanent change. |

## Statistics

### `pihole_stats_summary`

*read-only · structured output*

Overview of Pi-hole activity: queries, blocking rate, active clients, gravity size, and top query types. Start here for a health check.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `detail` | string | No | Response detail: minimal, normal (default), or full. |

### `pihole_stats_top_domains`

*read-only · structured output*

Top queried or top blocked domains ranked by count. Returns 10 by default, max 50.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `blocked` | boolean | No | True for top blocked, false/omit for top permitted. |
| `count` | number | No | Number of results (default 10, max 50). |
| `format` | string | No | Output format: text (default) or csv. |

### `pihole_stats_top_clients`

*read-only · structured output*

Most active network clients ranked by query count. Use blocked=true for clients with most blocked queries.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `blocked` | boolean | No | True for most-blocked clients. |
| `count` | number | No | Number of results (default 10, max 50). |
| `format` | string | No | Output format: text (default) or csv. |

### `pihole_stats_upstreams`

*read-only*

Upstream DNS server performance: query counts, average response time, and standard deviation per server.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `format` | string | No | Output format: text (default) or csv. |

### `pihole_stats_query_types`

*read-only*

Distribution of DNS query types (A, AAAA, MX, PTR, HTTPS, etc.) with counts.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `format` | string | No | Output format: text (default) or csv. |

### `pihole_stats_recent_blocked`

*read-only*

Most recently blocked domains — useful for spotting new tracking domains or false positives in real-time.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `count` | number | No | Number of domains (default 10). |
| `format` | string | No | Output format: text (default) or csv. |

### `pihole_stats_database`

*read-only*

Long-term database statistics for a time range. Returns totals for queries, blocked, and clients.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `from` | number | No | Start Unix timestamp. |
| `until` | number | No | End Unix timestamp. |

### `pihole_stats_database_top_domains`

*read-only*

Top queried or blocked domains from the long-term database for a date range. Returns 10 by default, max 50.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `blocked` | boolean | No | True for top blocked, false/omit for top permitted. |
| `count` | number | No | Number of results (default 10, max 50). |
| `format` | string | No | Output format: text (default) or csv. |
| `from` | number | No | Start Unix timestamp. |
| `until` | number | No | End Unix timestamp. |

### `pihole_stats_database_top_clients`

*read-only*

Most active clients from the long-term database for a date range. Use blocked=true for clients with most blocked queries.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `blocked` | boolean | No | True for most-blocked clients. |
| `count` | number | No | Number of results (default 10, max 50). |
| `format` | string | No | Output format: text (default) or csv. |
| `from` | number | No | Start Unix timestamp. |
| `until` | number | No | End Unix timestamp. |

### `pihole_stats_database_upstreams`

*read-only*

Historical upstream DNS server performance for a date range: query counts and average response times.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `format` | string | No | Output format: text (default) or csv. |
| `from` | number | No | Start Unix timestamp. |
| `until` | number | No | End Unix timestamp. |

### `pihole_stats_database_query_types`

*read-only*

Historical distribution of DNS query types (A, AAAA, MX, etc.) for a date range.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `from` | number | No | Start Unix timestamp. |
| `until` | number | No | End Unix timestamp. |

## System Info

### `pihole_info_system`

*read-only · structured output*

System health: hostname, OS, CPU/memory/disk usage, load averages, temperature, and DNS service status.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `detail` | string | No | Response detail: minimal, normal (default), or full. |

### `pihole_info_version`

*read-only*

Pi-hole component versions: core, FTL engine, web interface, and Docker tag if applicable.

_No parameters._

### `pihole_info_database`

*read-only*

Query database details: file size, total stored queries, and SQLite version.

_No parameters._

### `pihole_info_messages`

*read-only*

FTL diagnostic messages — warnings about DNS resolution failures, database issues, or configuration problems.

_No parameters._

### `pihole_info_dismiss_message`

*destructive*

Dismiss an FTL diagnostic message by ID, clearing it from Pi-hole. Get IDs from pihole_info_messages.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `id` | number | Yes | Message ID, as reported by pihole_info_messages. |

### `pihole_info_client`

*read-only*

Information about the requesting client's IP address and connection. Does not require authentication.

_No parameters._

### `pihole_info_ftl`

*read-only*

FTL engine process info: PID, privacy level, client and domain counts, and database query total.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `detail` | string | No | Response detail: minimal, normal (default), or full. |

### `pihole_info_metrics`

*read-only*

Live DNS and DHCP operational metrics including cache contents, reply counts, and lease statistics.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `detail` | string | No | Response detail: minimal, normal (default), or full. |

### `pihole_info_sensors`

*read-only*

Hardware temperature sensors with names, values, units, and paths.

_No parameters._

## Query Log

### `pihole_queries_search`

*read-only*

Search DNS query log with filters by domain, client, type, status, and time range. Returns 25 most recent by default with cursor pagination.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `client_ip` | string | No | Client IP filter (wildcards supported). |
| `client_name` | string | No | Client hostname filter. |
| `cursor` | number | No | Cursor from previous response for next page. |
| `detail` | string | No | Response detail: minimal, normal (default), or full. |
| `dnssec` | string | No | DNSSEC status: SECURE, INSECURE, etc. |
| `domain` | string | No | Domain filter (wildcards * supported). |
| `format` | string | No | Output format: text (default) or csv. |
| `from` | number | No | Start Unix timestamp. |
| `length` | number | No | Results per page (default 25, max 100). |
| `reply` | string | No | Reply type: NODATA, NXDOMAIN, IP, etc. |
| `status` | string | No | Status: GRAVITY, FORWARDED, CACHE, etc. |
| `type` | string | No | Query type: A, AAAA, MX, etc. |
| `until` | number | No | End Unix timestamp. |
| `upstream` | string | No | Upstream server filter. |

### `pihole_queries_suggestions`

*read-only*

Available filter values for pihole_queries_search: known domains, clients, types, statuses, and reply types.

_No parameters._

## History

### `pihole_history_graph`

*read-only*

In-memory query activity (FTL memory, last ~24h): total/cached/blocked/forwarded per slot. For arbitrary date ranges, use pihole_history_database.

_No parameters._

### `pihole_history_clients`

*read-only*

In-memory per-client query activity (FTL memory, last ~24h). For arbitrary date ranges, use pihole_history_database_clients.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `count` | number | No | Max clients to return (default 10, 0=all). |

### `pihole_history_database`

*read-only*

Long-term query activity from the FTL database (durable, arbitrary date range). Returns time-bucketed totals for total/cached/blocked/forwarded queries.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `from` | number | No | Start Unix timestamp (default: 7 days ago). |
| `until` | number | No | End Unix timestamp (default: now). |

### `pihole_history_database_clients`

*read-only*

Long-term per-client query activity from the FTL database (durable, arbitrary date range).

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `from` | number | No | Start Unix timestamp (default: 7 days ago). |
| `until` | number | No | End Unix timestamp (default: now). |

## Search

### `pihole_search_domains`

*read-only*

Search for a domain across all allow/deny lists and gravity blocklists. Use before modifying lists to check current state.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `domain` | string | Yes | Domain to search for. |
| `max_results` | number | No | Max results per category (default 20). |
| `partial` | boolean | No | Enable partial/substring matching (default false). |

## Domains

### `pihole_domains_list`

*read-only · structured output*

List domains on allow/deny lists. Filter by type (allow/deny) and kind (exact/regex). Use pihole_search_domains for cross-list search.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `detail` | string | No | Response detail: minimal, normal (default), or full. |
| `format` | string | No | Output format: text (default) or csv. |
| `kind` | string | No | Filter: 'exact' or 'regex'. |
| `type` | string | No | Filter: 'allow' or 'deny'. |

### `pihole_domains_add`

*destructive*

Add domains to an allow or deny list. Supports bulk add via comma-separated domains. Use pihole_search_domains first to avoid duplicates.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `type` | string | Yes | 'allow' or 'deny'. |
| `kind` | string | Yes | 'exact' or 'regex'. |
| `domain` | string | Yes | Domain(s) to add (comma-separated for bulk). |
| `comment` | string | No | Comment for the entry. |
| `enabled` | boolean | No | Enabled state (default true). |

### `pihole_domains_update`

*destructive*

Update a domain entry's comment, enabled status, or move it between allow/deny lists.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `type` | string | Yes | Current type: 'allow' or 'deny'. |
| `kind` | string | Yes | Current kind: 'exact' or 'regex'. |
| `domain` | string | Yes | Domain to update. |
| `comment` | string | No | Updated comment. |
| `enabled` | boolean | No | Updated enabled status. |

### `pihole_domains_delete`

*destructive*

Remove a domain from an allow or deny list. Requires the exact type, kind, and domain.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `type` | string | Yes | 'allow' or 'deny'. |
| `kind` | string | Yes | 'exact' or 'regex'. |
| `domain` | string | Yes | Domain to remove. |

### `pihole_domains_batch_delete`

*destructive*

Remove multiple domains at once. Each item needs domain, type (allow/deny), and kind (exact/regex).

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `items` | string | Yes | JSON array: [{"item":"domain","type":"deny","kind":"exact"}] |

## Groups

### `pihole_groups_list`

*read-only*

List groups used for organising domains and clients into sets with independent blocking rules.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `name` | string | No | Specific group name to look up. |

### `pihole_groups_add`

*destructive*

Create a group for organising domains and clients into sets with independent blocking rules.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `name` | string | Yes | Group name. |
| `comment` | string | No | Optional comment. |
| `enabled` | boolean | No | Enabled state (default true). |

### `pihole_groups_update`

*destructive*

Update or rename a group. Changing the name updates all associated domain and client references.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `name` | string | Yes | Current group name. |
| `comment` | string | No | Updated comment. |
| `enabled` | boolean | No | Updated enabled status. |
| `new_name` | string | No | New name (rename). |

### `pihole_groups_delete`

*destructive*

Delete a group by name. Domains and clients assigned to this group lose the association.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `name` | string | Yes | Group name to delete. |

### `pihole_groups_batch_delete`

*destructive*

Delete multiple groups at once. Provide a JSON array of group names.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `items` | string | Yes | JSON array: [{"item":"group_name"}] |

## Clients

### `pihole_clients_list`

*read-only*

List configured clients with their group assignments. Clients can be identified by IP, MAC, hostname, subnet, or interface.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `client` | string | No | Specific client to look up (IP, MAC, hostname). |
| `format` | string | No | Output format: text (default) or csv. |

### `pihole_clients_suggestions`

*read-only*

Unconfigured network clients seen by Pi-hole that don't have group assignments yet. Useful for discovering new devices.

_No parameters._

### `pihole_clients_add`

*destructive*

Add a client by IP, MAC, hostname, CIDR subnet, or interface name (prefixed with colon, e.g. :eth0).

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `client` | string | Yes | Client identifier. |
| `comment` | string | No | Optional comment. |

### `pihole_clients_update`

*destructive*

Update a configured client's comment or group assignments.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `client` | string | Yes | Client identifier. |
| `comment` | string | No | Updated comment. |

### `pihole_clients_delete`

*destructive*

Remove a configured client. The device remains on the network but loses group-based blocking rules.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `client` | string | Yes | Client identifier to remove. |

### `pihole_clients_batch_delete`

*destructive*

Remove multiple configured clients at once. Provide a JSON array of client identifiers.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `items` | string | Yes | JSON array of client identifiers, e.g. ["192.168.1.10","192.168.1.20"] |

## Lists

### `pihole_lists_list`

*read-only*

List configured blocklists and allowlists with domain counts and update status. Filter by type (allow/block).

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `detail` | string | No | Response detail: minimal, normal (default), or full. |
| `format` | string | No | Output format: text (default) or csv. |
| `type` | string | No | Filter: 'allow' or 'block'. |

### `pihole_lists_add`

*destructive*

Subscribe to a new blocklist or allowlist URL. Run pihole_action_gravity_update afterwards to download it.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `address` | string | Yes | URL of the list. |
| `type` | string | Yes | 'allow' or 'block'. |
| `comment` | string | No | Comment for the list. |
| `enabled` | boolean | No | Enabled state (default true). |

### `pihole_lists_update`

*destructive*

Update a blocklist or allowlist entry's comment, enabled status, or group assignments.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `address` | string | Yes | URL of the list. |
| `type` | string | Yes | 'allow' or 'block'. |
| `comment` | string | No | Updated comment. |
| `enabled` | boolean | No | Updated enabled status. |

### `pihole_lists_delete`

*destructive*

Unsubscribe from a blocklist or allowlist. Run pihole_action_gravity_update afterwards to apply changes.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `address` | string | Yes | URL to remove. |
| `type` | string | Yes | 'allow' or 'block'. |

### `pihole_lists_batch_delete`

*destructive*

Unsubscribe from multiple lists at once. Each item needs URL and type (allow/block).

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `items` | string | Yes | JSON array: [{"item":"url","type":"block"}] |

## Configuration

### `pihole_config_get`

*read-only*

Get Pi-hole configuration. Specify a section (dns, webserver, dhcp, etc.) for a subset, or omit for full config.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `detail` | string | No | Response detail: minimal, normal (default), or full. |
| `section` | string | No | Config section: dns, webserver, dhcp, files, misc, etc. |

### `pihole_config_set`

*destructive*

Modify Pi-hole configuration. Provide nested JSON properties to change. Changes take effect immediately and can affect DNS behaviour system-wide.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `config` | string | Yes | JSON config object, e.g. {"dns":{"blocking":{"active":true}}} |

### `pihole_config_get_value`

*read-only*

Get a specific configuration value by dotted path (e.g. dns.upstreams, webserver.port, dhcp.active).

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `element` | string | Yes | Config element path, e.g. dns.upstreams or dns/upstreams. |

### `pihole_config_add_value`

*destructive*

Add a value to a configuration array (e.g. add an upstream DNS server). Set restart=false to defer FTL restart.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `element` | string | Yes | Config element path, e.g. dns.upstreams. |
| `value` | string | Yes | Value to add. |
| `restart` | boolean | No | Restart FTL after change (default true). |

### `pihole_config_remove_value`

*destructive*

Remove a value from a configuration array (e.g. remove an upstream DNS server). Set restart=false to defer FTL restart.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `element` | string | Yes | Config element path, e.g. dns.upstreams. |
| `value` | string | Yes | Value to remove. |
| `restart` | boolean | No | Restart FTL after change (default true). |

### `pihole_config_properties`

*read-only*

List Pi-hole config keys that are read-only — set via pihole.toml or env var, not modifiable through the API. Use after a pihole_config_set error to confirm whether a key is intentionally locked.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `format` | string | No | Output format: text (default) or csv. |

## Actions

### `pihole_action_gravity_update`

*destructive*

Re-download all configured blocklists and rebuild the gravity database. Takes 30+ seconds. Run after adding or removing lists.

_No parameters._

### `pihole_action_restart_dns`

*destructive*

Restart the FTL DNS resolver. Briefly interrupts DNS resolution for all clients on the network.

_No parameters._

### `pihole_action_flush_logs`

*destructive*

Permanently delete all DNS logs — purges last 24 hours from memory and database. Irreversible.

_No parameters._

### `pihole_action_flush_network`

*destructive*

Permanently delete all network device records and associated addresses. Devices will be re-discovered over time.

_No parameters._

## Network

### `pihole_network_devices`

*read-only*

Devices seen on the network: MAC, IPs, hostnames, vendor, query count, and first/last seen timestamps. Returns 20 by default.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `detail` | string | No | Response detail: minimal, normal (default), or full. |
| `format` | string | No | Output format: text (default) or csv. |
| `max_addresses` | number | No | Max IPs per device (default 3). |
| `max_devices` | number | No | Max devices (default 20). |

### `pihole_network_gateway`

*read-only*

Network gateway details: address, interface, address family, and local interface IPs.

_No parameters._

### `pihole_network_info`

*read-only*

Lightweight summary of routing table and interface state. For detailed per-route or per-interface data, use pihole_network_routes or pihole_network_interfaces.

_No parameters._

### `pihole_network_routes`

*read-only*

System routing table entries with family, scope, source, destination, gateway, and outbound interface.

_No parameters._

### `pihole_network_interfaces`

*read-only*

Network interfaces with addresses, link state, speed, and traffic counters.

_No parameters._

### `pihole_network_delete_device`

*destructive*

Permanently delete a network device record by ID. Use pihole_network_devices first to find the ID. Devices may reappear if active on the network.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `id` | number | Yes | Numeric device ID from pihole_network_devices output. |

## DHCP

### `pihole_dhcp_leases`

*read-only*

Active DHCP leases: IP, hostname, MAC address, and expiry. Empty if Pi-hole's DHCP server is disabled.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `format` | string | No | Output format: text (default) or csv. |

### `pihole_dhcp_delete_lease`

*destructive*

Remove an active DHCP lease by IP address. Only works when Pi-hole's DHCP server is enabled.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `ip` | string | Yes | IP address of the lease to remove. |

## Logs

### `pihole_logs_dns`

*read-only*

DNS resolver (dnsmasq) log. Use next_id for incremental polling to follow new entries.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `next_id` | number | No | Only return lines after this ID (incremental polling). |

### `pihole_logs_ftl`

*read-only*

FTL engine log — internal Pi-hole diagnostics, database operations, and resolver events.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `next_id` | number | No | Only return lines after this ID (incremental polling). |

### `pihole_logs_webserver`

*read-only*

Web server access log — HTTP requests to the Pi-hole admin interface and API.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `next_id` | number | No | Only return lines after this ID (incremental polling). |

## Teleporter

### `pihole_teleporter_export`

*read-only*

Export a full Pi-hole configuration backup as a zip archive. Returns the saved file path and size.

_No parameters._

### `pihole_teleporter_import`

*destructive*

Import a Pi-hole configuration backup from a zip archive. Selectively import config, gravity tables, and DHCP leases.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `file_path` | string | Yes | Absolute path to the backup zip file. |
| `config` | boolean | No | Import Pi-hole configuration (default true). |
| `dhcp_leases` | boolean | No | Import DHCP leases (default true). |
| `gravity` | boolean | No | Import gravity database tables (default true). |

## Sessions

### `pihole_auth_sessions`

*read-only*

List active API sessions: remote address, user agent, and expiry. Useful for detecting unauthorised access.

_No parameters._

### `pihole_auth_revoke_session`

*destructive*

Revoke an active API session by ID. Cannot revoke the current session used by this server.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `id` | number | Yes | Session ID to revoke. |

## Multi-Instance

### `pihole_instance_diff`

*read-only · structured output*

Compare configuration between two Pi-hole instances (adlists, allow/deny rules, groups, clients, local DNS). Read-only — reports what a sync would change.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `target` | string | Yes | Instance compared against the source. |
| `categories` | string | No | Comma-separated categories to compare. Default: all of groups, lists, domains, clients, local_dns, cname. Host-specific and secret config (DHCP, interfaces, passwords, TLS) is never synced. |
| `detail` | string | No | Response detail: minimal, normal (default), or full. |
| `source` | string | No | Source-of-truth instance name. Default: the first configured instance. |

### `pihole_instance_sync`

*destructive · structured output*

Reconcile a target Pi-hole towards a source. Runs as a dry-run plan by default; re-call with mode=apply and the returned confirm_token to apply. Pushes one direction only (source to target).

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `target` | string | Yes | Instance to update. Only this instance is written to. |
| `categories` | string | No | Comma-separated categories to compare. Default: all of groups, lists, domains, clients, local_dns, cname. Host-specific and secret config (DHCP, interfaces, passwords, TLS) is never synced. |
| `confirm_token` | string | No | Token returned by a plan; required for mode=apply. |
| `mode` | string | No | 'plan' (default, dry-run) or 'apply'. |
| `prune` | boolean | No | Also delete target entries missing from the source (default false — add/update only). |
| `snapshot` | boolean | No | Export a teleporter backup of the target before applying (default true). |
| `source` | string | No | Source-of-truth instance name. Default: the first configured instance. |
